findings.detail: Holds conflicting access pair [PROD_DB + SIEM] — this violates separation of duties. findings.detail: Holds conflicting access pair [AWS_IAM + ADMIN_SYS] — this violates separation of duties. With AWS_IAM access, the user can modify data; with ADMIN_SYS, they can alter audit logs that would record those modifications, creating an undetectable fraud pathway. Violates NIST AC-5, SOX Sec.404, and PCI-DSS Req.6.4.
findings.recommendation: Remove access to one of (AWS_IAM, ADMIN_SYS) immediately; if business critical, add compensating control with dual-approval workflow and enhanced SIEM monitoring; document in GRC system as accepted risk with executive sign-off

suggested_actions: Continue automated monitoring — no immediate action required

findings.detail: Admin account inactive 24 days (4d beyond the 20d threshold). Privileged accounts left unmonitored create an undetected attack window — a threat actor could use these credentials for weeks without triggering activity-based alerts. Access to 7 system(s) amplifies the blast radius.
findings.recommendation: Cross-reference with HR payroll system immediately; if employee departed, revoke all access within 2 hours; if on leave, implement temporary access suspension with manager approval; document in identity governance system

findings.detail: Holds conflicting access pair [SIEM + AD] — this violates separation of duties. With SIEM access, the user can modify data; with AD, they can alter audit logs that would record those modifications, creating an undetectable fraud pathway. Violates NIST AC-5, SOX Sec.404, and PCI-DSS Req.6.4.
findings.recommendation: Remove access to one of (SIEM, AD) immediately; if business critical, add compensating control with dual-approval workflow and enhanced SIEM monitoring; document in GRC system as accepted risk with executive sign-off

findings.detail: Standard user performing admin_operation on critical resource PROD_DB (high sensitivity) in Sales department. Admin and SQL operations on critical systems require elevated privilege — this does not match a standard user role entitlement.
findings.recommendation: Validate business justification with Sales manager; raise access review ticket; verify no privilege escalation occurred

findings.detail: Standard user performing admin_operation on critical resource Admin_Console (medium sensitivity) in Marketing department. Admin and SQL operations on critical systems require elevated privilege — this does not match a standard user role entitlement.

findings.detail: Standard user performing admin_operation on critical resource HRIS (high sensitivity) in Finance department. Admin and SQL operations on critical systems require elevated privilege — this does not match a standard user role entitlement.
findings.recommendation: Validate business justification with Finance manager; raise access review ticket; verify no privilege escalation occurred

findings.detail: admin_operation on HRIS (high sensitivity) during unusual_hours by user in Finance — high-risk operation on sensitive resource outside business hours requires approved change window.
findings.recommendation: Cross-check ServiceNow change calendar; verify on-call roster; escalate to SOC if unexplained

findings.detail: Isolation Forest anomaly score 89/100 — statistically unusual pattern for this user/resource/time combination
findings.recommendation: Review in context of 30-day baseline; mark FP if legitimate activity

findings.detail: Export from HRIS (HIGH sensitivity) — high-sensitivity data export requires DLP compliance review
findings.recommendation: Verify DLP policy compliance; confirm data destination and recipient; check approval workflow; review against authorised export schedule

descriptions: On-prem AD: all user accounts & credentials
ITSM: change requests, CI/CD configs
Cloud IAM: full AWS resource access
Production DB: customer PII & financial records
Full system admin: install, create accounts

system_impacts.desc: Production DB: customer PII & financial records
Full system admin: install, create accounts
Security logs: attacker can disable alerts
Cloud IAM: full AWS resource access
GCP: Cloud Storage, BigQuery, Compute
CRM: customer contacts, deals, pipeline
SSO hub: lateral movement to all connected apps
Full internal network access
Cloud identity: SSO sessions, MFA configs

dlp_rules.name: Block PROD_DB access for stale admins
Quarantine SoD violators from critical resources
dlp_rules.trigger: SOD_VIOLATION AND resource IN (PROD_DB, Admin_Console, SIEM)

dlp_rules.name: Deep scan all bulk exports from Data_Lake

A standard user role should not have admin-level system access — this pattern matches insider threat and privilege escalation indicators.

steps.verify: Ticket resolved with disposition and action taken

steps.verify: DPO notified; GDPR Art.33 72h clock started if personal data

playbooks.title: After-Hours High-Risk Access Investigation
playbooks.finding_detail: 2 high-risk action(s) on sensitive resources during off-hours (GL_System, PROD_DB) — possible unauthorized access or compromised account

steps.detail: Verify if approved change window covers this timeframe in ServiceNow
steps.verify: Change record found OR no record (escalate immediately)

steps.detail: Confirm user was scheduled on-call during the off-hours access period
steps.verify: On-call roster confirms or denies; documented

steps.detail: Call/message user to verify activity was intentional and authorised
steps.verify: User confirms activity OR cannot be reached (escalate)

steps.detail: Cross-correlate with VPN logs, badge access, geographic location of source IP
steps.verify: No anomalous geo-indicators OR suspicious — escalate to CISO

steps.detail: If suspicious: isolate account, initiate IR process. If clean: document, close, tune rule
steps.verify: Ticket closed with disposition: LEGITIMATE or INCIDENT

playbooks.title: Stale Privileged Account Remediation
playbooks.finding_detail: Power User account inactive 46 days (16d beyond the 30d threshold). Privileged accounts left unmonitored create an undetected attack window — a threat actor could use these credentials for weeks without triggering activity-based alerts. Access to 3 system(s) amplifies the blast radius.

steps.detail: Check HRIS for active employment; confirm user has not departed
steps.verify: HR confirms active/inactive status in ticket

steps.detail: Email manager to confirm role still requires power-user access to 3 systems
steps.verify: Manager email confirmation attached

steps.detail: If no manager response in 8h: disable account in Azure_AD, Salesforce, SIEM pending review
steps.verify: Account suspended; monitoring alert configured

steps.detail: Remove excess 0 systems; retain only role-required access
steps.verify: Access profile updated; peer review completed

steps.detail: Review audit logs for suspicious activity during stale period; check for lateral movement
steps.verify: Audit report filed; no unauthorized activity found

steps.detail: Analyse the specific risk indicators identified for jacob.patel
steps.verify: Risk context documented in ticket

steps.detail: Check HR records, manager confirmation, and business justification
steps.verify: Context gathered; risk rating confirmed or revised

steps.detail: Apply appropriate remediation action based on confirmed risk level
steps.verify: Ticket resolved with disposition and action taken

playbooks.title: Separation of Duties Violation Remediation
playbooks.finding_detail: Holds conflicting access pair [PROD_DB + ADMIN_SYS] — this violates separation of duties. With PROD_DB access, the user can modify data; with ADMIN_SYS, they can alter audit logs that would record those modifications, creating an undetectable fraud pathway. Violates NIST AC-5, SOX Sec.404, and PCI-DSS Req.6.4.

steps.detail: Record PROD_DB + ADMIN_SYS conflict in GRC system with risk rating HIGH
steps.verify: GRC risk record created with ticket reference

steps.detail: Business owner: accept risk with compensating controls OR remove one access right
steps.verify: Signed decision record filed

steps.detail: Remove lower-priority access (ADMIN_SYS); update role assignment in identity system
steps.verify: User no longer holds both conflicting rights; SoD check passes

steps.detail: If risk accepted: enable enhanced SIEM monitoring; require dual approval for sensitive ops
steps.verify: SIEM alert rule active; approval workflow enabled and tested

playbooks.title: Separation of Duties Violation Remediation
playbooks.finding_detail: Holds conflicting access pair [PROD_DB + ADMIN_SYS] — this violates separation of duties. With PROD_DB access, the user can modify data; with ADMIN_SYS, they can alter audit logs that would record those modifications, creating an undetectable fraud pathway. Violates NIST AC-5, SOX Sec.404, and PCI-DSS Req.6.4.

playbooks.title: Stale Privileged Account Remediation
playbooks.finding_detail: Admin account inactive 34 days (14d beyond the 20d threshold). steps.verify: Account suspended; monitoring alert configured

steps.detail: Remove excess 0 systems; retain only role-required access
steps.verify: Access profile updated; peer review completed

steps.detail: Review audit logs for suspicious activity during stale period; check for lateral movement
steps.verify: Audit report filed; no unauthorized activity found

steps.detail: Remove excess 0 systems; retain only role-required access

steps.verify: Audit report filed; no unauthorized activity found

playbooks.title: Separation of Duties Violation Remediation
playbooks.finding_detail: Holds conflicting access pair [Azure_AD + PROD_DB] — this violates separation of duties. With Azure_AD access, the user can modify data; with PROD_DB, they can alter audit logs that would record those modifications, creating an undetectable fraud pathway. steps.detail: Record Azure_AD + VPN conflict in GRC system with risk rating HIGH

steps.detail: Business owner: accept risk with compensating controls OR remove one access right

steps.detail: Remove lower-priority access (VPN); update role assignment in identity system
steps.verify: User no longer holds both conflicting rights; SoD check passes

steps.detail: If risk accepted: enable enhanced SIEM monitoring; require dual approval for sensitive ops
steps.verify: SIEM alert rule active; approval workflow enabled and tested

playbooks.title: Stale Privileged Account Remediation
playbooks.finding_detail: Power User account inactive 49 days (19d beyond the 30d threshold). Privileged accounts left unmonitored create an undetected attack window — a threat actor could use these credentials for weeks without triggering activity-based alerts. Access to 2 system(s) amplifies the blast radius.

steps.detail: Check HRIS for active employment; confirm user has not departed
steps.verify: HR confirms active/inactive status in ticket

steps.detail: Email manager to confirm role still requires power-user access to 2 systems
steps.verify: Manager email confirmation attached

steps.detail: If no manager response in 8h: disable account in Okta, AWS_IAM pending review
steps.verify: Account suspended; monitoring alert configured

steps.detail: Remove excess 0 systems; retain only role-required access
steps.verify: Access profile updated; peer review completed

steps.detail: Review audit logs for suspicious activity during stale period; check for lateral movement
steps.verify: Audit report filed; no unauthorized activity found

playbooks.title: MFA Enforcement for Privileged Account
playbooks.finding_detail: power-user account has no MFA-capable system (Okta/Azure_AD) in access list — admin actions unprotected by second factor

steps.detail: Enroll richard.yang in Okta or Azure AD MFA; send enrollment link
steps.verify: MFA enrollment invitation sent and acknowledged

steps.detail: Enable conditional access policy requiring MFA for all power-user actions
steps.verify: Conditional access policy active; tested with test account

steps.detail: If not enrolled in 24h: block privileged system access; escalate to manager
steps.verify: User enrolled or access blocked; manager notified

playbooks.title: Separation of Duties Violation Remediation
playbooks.finding_detail: Holds conflicting access pair [AWS_IAM + ADMIN_SYS] — this violates separation of duties. With AWS_IAM access, the user can modify data; with ADMIN_SYS, they can alter audit logs that would record those modifications, creating an undetectable fraud pathway. Violates NIST AC-5, SOX Sec.404, and PCI-DSS Req.6.4.

steps.detail: Record ADMIN_SYS + ServiceNow conflict in GRC system with risk rating HIGH
steps.verify: GRC risk record created with ticket reference

steps.detail: Business owner: accept risk with compensating controls OR remove one access right
steps.verify: Signed decision record filed

steps.detail: Remove lower-priority access (ServiceNow); update role assignment in identity system
steps.verify: User no longer holds both conflicting rights; SoD check passes

steps.detail: If risk accepted: enable enhanced SIEM monitoring; require dual approval for sensitive ops
steps.verify: SIEM alert rule active; approval workflow enabled and tested

playbooks.title: Bulk Data Export Investigation
playbooks.finding_detail: 2 export events (2 on high-sensitivity resources including PROD_DB, GL_System) in analysis window

steps.detail: Check DLP system for export destination, file type, data classification, volume
steps.verify: DLP destination confirmed; data classification known

steps.detail: Temporarily revoke export_data permission for richard.yang pending investigation
steps.verify: Export capability suspended; SIEM alert active

steps.detail: Inform CISO and DPO of potential exfiltration from 2 export events (2 on high-sensitivity r
steps.verify: DPO notified; GDPR Art.33 72h clock started if personal data

steps.detail: Retrieve full export logs; determine exact volume, destination endpoint, and content
steps.verify: Forensic report drafted; exfiltration confirmed or ruled out

steps.detail: Check HRIS for active employment; confirm user has not departed

steps.detail: Review audit logs for suspicious activity during stale period; check for lateral movement

playbooks.title: Separation of Duties Violation Remediation
playbooks.finding_detail: Holds conflicting access pair [PROD_DB + ADMIN_SYS] — this violates separation of duties. With PROD_DB access, the user can modify data; with ADMIN_SYS, they can alter audit logs that would record those modifications, creating an undetectable fraud pathway. Violates NIST AC-5, SOX Sec.404, and PCI-DSS Req.6.4.

steps.detail: Record PROD_DB + AD conflict in GRC system with risk rating HIGH
steps.verify: GRC risk record created with ticket reference

steps.detail: Business owner: accept risk with compensating controls OR remove one access right
steps.verify: Signed decision record filed

steps.detail: Remove lower-priority access (AD); update role assignment in identity system
steps.verify: User no longer holds both conflicting rights; SoD check passes

steps.detail: If risk accepted: enable enhanced SIEM monitoring; require dual approval for sensitive ops
steps.verify: SIEM alert rule active; approval workflow enabled and tested

playbooks.title: After-Hours High-Risk Access Investigation
playbooks.finding_detail: 2 high-risk action(s) on sensitive resources during off-hours (GL_System, Customer_Vault) — possible unauthorized access or compromised account

steps.detail: Verify if approved change window covers this timeframe in ServiceNow

steps.detail: Confirm user was scheduled on-call during the off-hours access period

steps.detail: Call/message user to verify activity was intentional and authorised
steps.verify: User confirms activity OR cannot be reached (escalate)

steps.detail: Cross-correlate with VPN logs, badge access, geographic location of source IP
steps.verify: No anomalous geo-indicators OR suspicious — escalate to CISO

steps.detail: If suspicious: isolate account, initiate IR process. If clean: document, close, tune rule
steps.verify: Ticket closed with disposition: LEGITIMATE or INCIDENT

playbooks.title: Stale Privileged Account Remediation
playbooks.finding_detail: Power User account inactive 51 days (21d beyond the 30d threshold). Privileged accounts left unmonitored create an undetected attack window — a threat actor could use these credentials for weeks without triggering activity-based alerts. Access to 4 system(s) amplifies the blast radius.

steps.detail: Check HRIS for active employment; confirm user has not departed
steps.verify: HR confirms active/inactive status in ticket

steps.detail: Email manager to confirm role still requires power-user access to 4 systems
steps.verify: Manager email confirmation attached

steps.detail: If no manager response in 8h: disable account in SIEM, VPN, PROD_DB, Okta pending review
steps.verify: Account suspended; monitoring alert configured

steps.detail: Remove excess 0 systems; retain only role-required access
steps.verify: Access profile updated; peer review completed

steps.detail: Review audit logs for suspicious activity during stale period; check for lateral movement
steps.verify: Audit report filed; no unauthorized activity found

playbooks.title: Separation of Duties Violation Remediation
playbooks.finding_detail: Holds conflicting access pair [PROD_DB + SIEM] — this violates separation of duties. With PROD_DB access, the user can modify data; with SIEM, they can alter audit logs that would record those modifications, creating an undetectable fraud pathway. Violates NIST AC-5, SOX Sec.404, and PCI-DSS Req.6.4.

steps.detail: Record SIEM + VPN conflict in GRC system with risk rating HIGH
steps.verify: GRC risk record created with ticket reference

steps.detail: Business owner: accept risk with compensating controls OR remove one access right
steps.verify: Signed decision record filed

steps.detail: Remove lower-priority access (VPN); update role assignment in identity system
steps.verify: User no longer holds both conflicting rights; SoD check passes

steps.detail: If risk accepted: enable enhanced SIEM monitoring; require dual approval for sensitive ops
steps.verify: SIEM alert rule active; approval workflow enabled and tested

playbooks.title: Security Risk Investigation
playbooks.finding_detail: Standard user in Operations (Lead) holds access to critical identity systems: ADMIN_SYS. These systems control authentication, authorisation, and audit logs. A standard user role should not have admin-level system access — this pattern matches insider threat and privilege escalation indicators.

steps.detail: Analyse the specific risk indicators identified for gary.kang
steps.verify: Risk context documented in ticket

steps.detail: Check HR records, manager confirmation, and business justification
steps.verify: Context gathered; risk rating confirmed or revised

steps.detail: Apply appropriate remediation action based on confirmed risk level
steps.verify: Ticket resolved with disposition and action taken

playbooks.title: Stale Service Account Decommissioning
playbooks.finding_detail: Service account inactive 53 days — 28d beyond the 25d service account threshold. Inactive service accounts often indicate decommissioned applications whose credentials were never revoked, creating a persistent backdoor that is difficult to detect through normal monitoring.

steps.detail: Trace service account to application via CMDB; notify owning team
steps.verify: Owner team identified and notified via ticket

steps.detail: Scan CI/CD pipelines, cron jobs, and API configs for references to this account
steps.verify: Dependency list confirmed; zero active callers found

steps.detail: Rotate password/API key first to surface any hidden consumers before disabling
steps.verify: No new auth failures observed after rotation in monitoring window

steps.detail: Disable account; monitor 48h for auth failures before permanent deletion
steps.verify: Zero auth failures observed; account safe to delete

steps.detail: Delete account; update CMDB with decommission date, owner, and replacement reference
steps.verify: Account removed from all systems; CMDB record updated

executive_summary: Societe Generale's internal security assessment reveals alarming risks across our user base, with 85 accounts classified as CRITICAL and 53 Separation of Duty (SoD) violations identified. The elevated risk of data loss due to potential breaches threatens the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of our sensitive records, with 10 exposed accounts posing a risk to millions of customer data. Despite a high user detection precision, there are still opportunities for attackers to evade detection. We must take urgent action to mitigate these risks, particularly with the top 3 highest-risk accounts, which are associated with user privileges. The Compliance and Security teams must collaborate to review and remediate these vulnerabilities to minimize exposure and ensure uninterrupted operations.
